plan_review_notes: Approve this MCP-first inspection task as an architecture/discovery slice: verify that repo MCP resources, core docs, README, init-mcp.sh, and the evolution code path were inspected, then record the concrete follow-up work items that were executed from that context.

plan_review_history.notes: Approve this MCP-first inspection task as an architecture/discovery slice: verify that repo MCP resources, core docs, README, init-mcp.sh, and the evolution code path were inspected, then record the concrete follow-up work items that were executed from that context.

spec_review_notes: Freeze scope to MCP-first inspection and decision capture only. This task is satisfied by documenting the architecture findings and the concrete self-evolution follow-up slices that were subsequently executed; it does not need its own separate feature code path.

normal_cases.name: Repo MCP resources and local docs are sufficient to identify the current control-plane architecture and the remaining self-evolution slices.
The concrete feasible slices discovered from that inspection are executed end-to-end as separate Sisyphus tasks.

edge_cases.name: The repository board remains green while the exploration task fans out into smaller implementation tasks that can be verified independently.

exception_cases.name: Duplicate or superseded task creation attempts are visible in the event feed and do not prevent the next valid follow-up tasks from being created and completed.

verification_methods.target: MCP resources and local docs identify the current architecture
verification_methods.method: manual review of repo://schema/mcp, repo://status/board, repo://status/conformance, repo://status/events, README.md, init-mcp.sh

verification_methods.target: Feasible follow-up slices are executed end-to-end
verification_methods.method: ./.venv/bin/python -m unittest tests.test_evolution -v` plus closed follow-up tasks for evolution core/dataset/harness/constraints/report

verification_methods.target: Duplicate task creation is visible and recoverable
